# CLI reference

One page per `pg_hardstorage` subcommand.  Pages are
generated from the Cobra command tree on every
`make docs-regen`; CI fails on drift.

| Command | Synopsis |
| --- | --- |
| [`pg_hardstorage`](pg_hardstorage.md) | PostgreSQL backup, done right. |
| [`pg_hardstorage agent`](pg_hardstorage_agent.md) | Run the host agent (loads config, runs scheduled backups + retention) |
| [`pg_hardstorage anomaly`](pg_hardstorage_anomaly.md) | Inspect backup metric baselines for outliers |
| [`pg_hardstorage anomaly check`](pg_hardstorage_anomaly_check.md) | Score the latest backup (or all backups with --all) against priors |
| [`pg_hardstorage approval`](pg_hardstorage_approval.md) | n-of-m approval workflow for destructive operations |
| [`pg_hardstorage approval approve`](pg_hardstorage_approval_approve.md) | Approve an outstanding request |
| [`pg_hardstorage approval list`](pg_hardstorage_approval_list.md) | List approval requests in the repo |
| [`pg_hardstorage approval purge-expired`](pg_hardstorage_approval_purge-expired.md) | Record expiry for approval requests past their TTL (audited) |
| [`pg_hardstorage approval request`](pg_hardstorage_approval_request.md) | Create a new n-of-m approval request |
| [`pg_hardstorage approval revoke`](pg_hardstorage_approval_revoke.md) | Revoke an outstanding request (cannot be approved further) |
| [`pg_hardstorage approval status`](pg_hardstorage_approval_status.md) | Show the current status of a request |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit`](pg_hardstorage_audit.md) | Query the hash-chained audit log |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit anchor`](pg_hardstorage_audit_anchor.md) | Publish the chain head into the transparency log |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit append`](pg_hardstorage_audit_append.md) | Record a new event in the audit chain |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit export-bundle`](pg_hardstorage_audit_export-bundle.md) | Export a signed evidence bundle of audit events |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit search`](pg_hardstorage_audit_search.md) | Query audit events with filters |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit summary`](pg_hardstorage_audit_summary.md) | Aggregate audit-event counts by action |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit verify-anchor`](pg_hardstorage_audit_verify-anchor.md) | Compare the named anchor against the local chain |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit verify-bundle`](pg_hardstorage_audit_verify-bundle.md) | Verify a signed audit evidence bundle |
| [`pg_hardstorage audit verify-chain`](pg_hardstorage_audit_verify-chain.md) | Walk the audit log and assert hash-chain integrity |
| [`pg_hardstorage backup`](pg_hardstorage_backup.md) | Take a backup of a deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage backup compare`](pg_hardstorage_backup_compare.md) | Diff two backups — file & chunk overlap, size delta, top-N changed files |
| [`pg_hardstorage backup delete`](pg_hardstorage_backup_delete.md) | Soft-delete a specific backup (chunks reclaimed by next repo gc) |
| [`pg_hardstorage backup graph`](pg_hardstorage_backup_graph.md) | Backup-chain topology + per-node dedup analysis |
| [`pg_hardstorage backup undelete`](pg_hardstorage_backup_undelete.md) | Resurrect one or more soft-deleted backups (removes tombstone marker) |
| [`pg_hardstorage capacity`](pg_hardstorage_capacity.md) | Projected repository size, WAL volume, and pre-flight free-space checks |
| [`pg_hardstorage capacity preflight`](pg_hardstorage_capacity_preflight.md) | Check whether the repo has free space for a projected backup |
| [`pg_hardstorage capacity report`](pg_hardstorage_capacity_report.md) | Projected repository size and WAL volume |
| [`pg_hardstorage changelog`](pg_hardstorage_changelog.md) | Show changelog |
| [`pg_hardstorage classify`](pg_hardstorage_classify.md) | Apply a data-classification tag to a deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage classify list`](pg_hardstorage_classify_list.md) | Show every deployment's classification |
| [`pg_hardstorage classify set`](pg_hardstorage_classify_set.md) | Tag a deployment with public / internal / confidential / restricted |
| [`pg_hardstorage compat`](pg_hardstorage_compat.md) | Compatibility helpers for legacy backup tools |
| [`pg_hardstorage compat translate`](pg_hardstorage_compat_translate.md) | Convert a legacy config to pg_hardstorage.yaml |
| [`pg_hardstorage completion`](pg_hardstorage_completion.md) | Generate shell completion scripts |
| [`pg_hardstorage compliance`](pg_hardstorage_compliance.md) | Compliance reporting (SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / HIPAA / PCI / FedRAMP-friendly) |
| [`pg_hardstorage compliance report`](pg_hardstorage_compliance_report.md) | Generate a time-windowed compliance report |
| [`pg_hardstorage cost`](pg_hardstorage_cost.md) | Per-deployment / per-tenant repository cost |
| [`pg_hardstorage cost report`](pg_hardstorage_cost_report.md) | Per-deployment / per-tenant repository cost |
| [`pg_hardstorage db`](pg_hardstorage_db.md) | In-database integration (SQL views, upsert helpers) |
| [`pg_hardstorage db install-extension`](pg_hardstorage_db_install-extension.md) | Install the pg_hardstorage in-DB extension (creates schema + tables + views + functions) |
| [`pg_hardstorage db uninstall-extension`](pg_hardstorage_db_uninstall-extension.md) | Remove the pg_hardstorage in-DB schema |
| [`pg_hardstorage demo`](pg_hardstorage_demo.md) | Run a throwaway end-to-end demo (init → backup → restore → verify) on a temporary PG in Docker |
| [`pg_hardstorage deployment`](pg_hardstorage_deployment.md) | Manage deployments (add/list/remove/edit/test) |
| [`pg_hardstorage deployment add`](pg_hardstorage_deployment_add.md) | Add a new deployment to the config |
| [`pg_hardstorage deployment edit`](pg_hardstorage_deployment_edit.md) | Update fields on an existing deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage deployment list`](pg_hardstorage_deployment_list.md) | List configured deployments |
| [`pg_hardstorage deployment remove`](pg_hardstorage_deployment_remove.md) | Remove a deployment from the config |
| [`pg_hardstorage deployment test`](pg_hardstorage_deployment_test.md) | Probe PG for a configured deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage doctor`](pg_hardstorage_doctor.md) | Run health checks and suggest fixes |
| [`pg_hardstorage dsa`](pg_hardstorage_dsa.md) | GDPR Data Subject Access helper: locate which backups contain a subject's data |
| [`pg_hardstorage dsa list`](pg_hardstorage_dsa_list.md) | List DSA reports newest-first |
| [`pg_hardstorage dsa locate`](pg_hardstorage_dsa_locate.md) | Generate a signed DSA report for a subject + tenant |
| [`pg_hardstorage dsa show`](pg_hardstorage_dsa_show.md) | Show one DSA report's full body + suggested actions |
| [`pg_hardstorage dsa verify`](pg_hardstorage_dsa_verify.md) | Re-validate the signature on a previously-stored DSA report |
| [`pg_hardstorage explain`](pg_hardstorage_explain.md) | Explain pg_hardstorage commands |
| [`pg_hardstorage fleet`](pg_hardstorage_fleet.md) | Fleet-wide queries across deployments and backups |
| [`pg_hardstorage fleet search`](pg_hardstorage_fleet_search.md) | Search every deployment's manifests in one repo |
| [`pg_hardstorage forecast`](pg_hardstorage_forecast.md) | Capacity-planning + cost-projection report |
| [`pg_hardstorage gameday`](pg_hardstorage_gameday.md) | Opt-in chaos automation |
| [`pg_hardstorage gameday list`](pg_hardstorage_gameday_list.md) | List registered scenarios |
| [`pg_hardstorage gameday report`](pg_hardstorage_gameday_report.md) | Aggregate recent gameday runs from the audit chain |
| [`pg_hardstorage gameday run`](pg_hardstorage_gameday_run.md) | Run one chaos scenario, return structured Pass/Fail |
| [`pg_hardstorage glossary`](pg_hardstorage_glossary.md) | Look up pg_hardstorage terminology |
| [`pg_hardstorage hold`](pg_hardstorage_hold.md) | Legal hold — pin backups against retention sweeps |
| [`pg_hardstorage hold add`](pg_hardstorage_hold_add.md) | Place a legal hold on a backup |
| [`pg_hardstorage hold list`](pg_hardstorage_hold_list.md) | List active legal holds |
| [`pg_hardstorage hold purge-expired`](pg_hardstorage_hold_purge-expired.md) | Remove every hold marker whose ExpiresAt is in the past |
| [`pg_hardstorage hold remove`](pg_hardstorage_hold_remove.md) | Release a legal hold (the only way to clear one) |
| [`pg_hardstorage init`](pg_hardstorage_init.md) | Day-0 setup wizard — connect, init repo, take first backup |
| [`pg_hardstorage insider`](pg_hardstorage_insider.md) | Insider-threat detection: scan the audit log for unusual patterns |
| [`pg_hardstorage insider list`](pg_hardstorage_insider_list.md) | List insider scans newest-first |
| [`pg_hardstorage insider scan`](pg_hardstorage_insider_scan.md) | Run one insider-threat scan; persist the result |
| [`pg_hardstorage insider show`](pg_hardstorage_insider_show.md) | Show one scan's full body + findings |
| [`pg_hardstorage integrity`](pg_hardstorage_integrity.md) | Continuous-attestation runs: re-verify manifests + chunks, sign the report |
| [`pg_hardstorage integrity list`](pg_hardstorage_integrity_list.md) | List integrity runs newest-first |
| [`pg_hardstorage integrity run`](pg_hardstorage_integrity_run.md) | Execute one continuous-attestation run; sign + persist; exit 9 on issues |
| [`pg_hardstorage integrity show`](pg_hardstorage_integrity_show.md) | Show one integrity run's full body + per-failure detail |
| [`pg_hardstorage integrity verify`](pg_hardstorage_integrity_verify.md) | Re-validate the signature on a previously-stored run |
| [`pg_hardstorage jit`](pg_hardstorage_jit.md) | Just-in-time access tokens for break-glass operations |
| [`pg_hardstorage jit issue`](pg_hardstorage_jit_issue.md) | Mint a new JIT access token |
| [`pg_hardstorage jit list`](pg_hardstorage_jit_list.md) | List JIT tokens with effective lifecycle status |
| [`pg_hardstorage jit revoke`](pg_hardstorage_jit_revoke.md) | Revoke a JIT token (writes a sibling .revoked marker) |
| [`pg_hardstorage jit show`](pg_hardstorage_jit_show.md) | Show one JIT token's full body + revocation status |
| [`pg_hardstorage jit verify`](pg_hardstorage_jit_verify.md) | Verify a supplied JIT token is currently valid for an operation |
| [`pg_hardstorage kms`](pg_hardstorage_kms.md) | Manage encryption keys |
| [`pg_hardstorage kms hsm-status`](pg_hardstorage_kms_hsm-status.md) | Report PKCS#11 / TPM 2.0 binding state (deferred) |
| [`pg_hardstorage kms inspect`](pg_hardstorage_kms_inspect.md) | Read-only summary of the keyring |
| [`pg_hardstorage kms rotate`](pg_hardstorage_kms_rotate.md) | Re-wrap every encrypted backup's DEK with a new KEK |
| [`pg_hardstorage kms shred`](pg_hardstorage_kms_shred.md) | Irreversibly destroy the local KEK (n-of-m approval REQUIRED) |
| [`pg_hardstorage kms verify`](pg_hardstorage_kms_verify.md) | Verify the encryption envelope on every backup manifest in the repo |
| [`pg_hardstorage lint`](pg_hardstorage_lint.md) | Validate pg_hardstorage.yaml |
| [`pg_hardstorage list`](pg_hardstorage_list.md) | List backups for a deployment, newest first |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm`](pg_hardstorage_llm.md) | LLM helper — grounded assistant with skill files, mandatory preview, full audit |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm ask`](pg_hardstorage_llm_ask.md) | One-shot question answering |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm chat`](pg_hardstorage_llm_chat.md) | Interactive chat session — REPL with /show-* transparency commands |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm doctor`](pg_hardstorage_llm_doctor.md) | Health check for the LLM helper subsystem |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm explain`](pg_hardstorage_llm_explain.md) | Explain a CLI invocation in plain English |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm export-session`](pg_hardstorage_llm_export-session.md) | Export every audit event for an LLM session as a signed evidence bundle |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm history`](pg_hardstorage_llm_history.md) | Inspect / shred encrypted LLM conversation transcripts |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm history list`](pg_hardstorage_llm_history_list.md) | List recorded sessions for the operator (or all principals with --principal '*') |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm history show`](pg_hardstorage_llm_history_show.md) | Decrypt and print one session's transcript |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm history shred`](pg_hardstorage_llm_history_shred.md) | Delete every session for the principal (irreversible without backups) |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm skill`](pg_hardstorage_llm_skill.md) | Manage LLM skill files |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm skill history`](pg_hardstorage_llm_skill_history.md) | List installed snapshots for a skill (most recent last) |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm skill install`](pg_hardstorage_llm_skill_install.md) | Install a skill YAML file into the operator overlay (snapshots any existing version for rollback) |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm skill lint`](pg_hardstorage_llm_skill_lint.md) | Validate a skill YAML file (or a loaded skill name) |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm skill list`](pg_hardstorage_llm_skill_list.md) | List loaded skills (precedence chain merged) |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm skill rollback`](pg_hardstorage_llm_skill_rollback.md) | Revert a skill to its previous installed version |
| [`pg_hardstorage llm skill show`](pg_hardstorage_llm_skill_show.md) | Show one loaded skill |
| [`pg_hardstorage logical`](pg_hardstorage_logical.md) | Configure logical decoding sinks alongside physical WAL streaming |
| [`pg_hardstorage logical add`](pg_hardstorage_logical_add.md) | Register a logical-decoding stream |
| [`pg_hardstorage logical list`](pg_hardstorage_logical_list.md) | List registered logical streams |
| [`pg_hardstorage logical remove`](pg_hardstorage_logical_remove.md) | Remove a registered logical stream |
| [`pg_hardstorage logical status`](pg_hardstorage_logical_status.md) | Report registry state for one or all streams |
| [`pg_hardstorage logical stream`](pg_hardstorage_logical_stream.md) | Run the long-lived consumer for a registered stream |
| [`pg_hardstorage logs`](pg_hardstorage_logs.md) | Tail the pg_hardstorage agent's systemd journal |
| [`pg_hardstorage manifest`](pg_hardstorage_manifest.md) | Inspect backup manifests |
| [`pg_hardstorage manifest show`](pg_hardstorage_manifest_show.md) | Show a single backup's full manifest |
| [`pg_hardstorage notify`](pg_hardstorage_notify.md) | Configure event sinks (slack, webhook, syslog, ...) |
| [`pg_hardstorage notify add`](pg_hardstorage_notify_add.md) | Add a sink to pg_hardstorage.yaml |
| [`pg_hardstorage notify list`](pg_hardstorage_notify_list.md) | List configured sinks |
| [`pg_hardstorage notify remove`](pg_hardstorage_notify_remove.md) | Remove a sink by name |
| [`pg_hardstorage partial`](pg_hardstorage_partial.md) | Table-level inspection (v0.1) and restore |
| [`pg_hardstorage partial dump`](pg_hardstorage_partial_dump.md) | Restore + start sandbox PG + pg_dump SQL for selected tables |
| [`pg_hardstorage partial inspect`](pg_hardstorage_partial_inspect.md) | Report which manifest entries a partial restore would touch |
| [`pg_hardstorage partial restore`](pg_hardstorage_partial_restore.md) | Extract specific tables' files from a backup into --target |
| [`pg_hardstorage patroni`](pg_hardstorage_patroni.md) | Read-only view of a Patroni-managed PostgreSQL cluster |
| [`pg_hardstorage patroni follow`](pg_hardstorage_patroni_follow.md) | Watch a Patroni cluster and stream leader-change events |
| [`pg_hardstorage patroni history`](pg_hardstorage_patroni_history.md) | Print Patroni's timeline-history events |
| [`pg_hardstorage patroni status`](pg_hardstorage_patroni_status.md) | Print the Patroni cluster topology + leader |
| [`pg_hardstorage plugin`](pg_hardstorage_plugin.md) | Discover and inspect Tier-2 plugins |
| [`pg_hardstorage plugin list`](pg_hardstorage_plugin_list.md) | List discovered Tier-2 plugins |
| [`pg_hardstorage recovery`](pg_hardstorage_recovery.md) | Recovery-toolkit: readiness scorecard, PITR windows, and more |
| [`pg_hardstorage recovery drill`](pg_hardstorage_recovery_drill.md) | Run a full restore-and-verify drill against a real backup |
| [`pg_hardstorage recovery drill history`](pg_hardstorage_recovery_drill_history.md) | List + summarise past drill runs (auto-recorded by `recovery drill`) |
| [`pg_hardstorage recovery readiness`](pg_hardstorage_recovery_readiness.md) | Single-screen recovery-readiness scorecard for one deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage recovery windows`](pg_hardstorage_recovery_windows.md) | List every PITR window available for a deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage redact`](pg_hardstorage_redact.md) | Post-restore PII redaction (rules-driven UPDATE statements) |
| [`pg_hardstorage redact apply`](pg_hardstorage_redact_apply.md) | Apply a redaction rules file to a connected PostgreSQL cluster |
| [`pg_hardstorage redact preview`](pg_hardstorage_redact_preview.md) | Preview the redaction plan without touching the database |
| [`pg_hardstorage repair`](pg_hardstorage_repair.md) | Targeted repair tools — explicit, idempotent, audited |
| [`pg_hardstorage repair attestation`](pg_hardstorage_repair_attestation.md) | Re-sign a manifest with the current keypair (audited) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repair chunks`](pg_hardstorage_repair_chunks.md) | Find chunks not referenced by any manifest, or manifests with missing chunks |
| [`pg_hardstorage repair index`](pg_hardstorage_repair_index.md) | Walk chunks/sha256/ and report the inventory |
| [`pg_hardstorage repair manifest`](pg_hardstorage_repair_manifest.md) | Reconcile a manifest's primary + replica copies (recover whichever side is missing/corrupt) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repair scrub`](pg_hardstorage_repair_scrub.md) | Verify chunk integrity (SHA-256 round-trip) across the repo |
| [`pg_hardstorage repair slot`](pg_hardstorage_repair_slot.md) | Recreate a missing replication slot (alias for `wal repair`) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo`](pg_hardstorage_repo.md) | Manage the repository |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo audit`](pg_hardstorage_repo_audit.md) | Comprehensive read-only repository state report |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo bundle`](pg_hardstorage_repo_bundle.md) | Air-gap export/import of repo state (manifests + chunks + WAL) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo bundle export`](pg_hardstorage_repo_bundle_export.md) | Export deployment state into an air-gap-transportable tar |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo bundle import`](pg_hardstorage_repo_bundle_import.md) | Import a previously-exported bundle into a destination repo (idempotent) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo check`](pg_hardstorage_repo_check.md) | Verify repository integrity (signatures + chunk references) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo compact`](pg_hardstorage_repo_compact.md) | Compact small chunks into pack files (deferred) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo gc`](pg_hardstorage_repo_gc.md) | Reclaim space — sweep chunks no manifest references |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo init`](pg_hardstorage_repo_init.md) | Create a new repository at the given URL |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo replicate`](pg_hardstorage_repo_replicate.md) | Copy committed manifests + chunks from a source repo to a replica repo |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo replicate verify`](pg_hardstorage_repo_replicate_verify.md) | Verify a replica repo is consistent with its source |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo scrub`](pg_hardstorage_repo_scrub.md) | Re-hash chunks to detect bit-rot (periodic maintenance) |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo set-mode`](pg_hardstorage_repo_set-mode.md) | Toggle the repository's write-access posture |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo usage`](pg_hardstorage_repo_usage.md) | Report repository usage by category |
| [`pg_hardstorage repo wipe`](pg_hardstorage_repo_wipe.md) | Permanently delete every object in the repo (n-of-m approval, or --force for non-WORM repos) |
| [`pg_hardstorage residency`](pg_hardstorage_residency.md) | Data-residency pinning for a deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage residency check`](pg_hardstorage_residency_check.md) | Verify the configured repo's region matches the deployment's residency policy |
| [`pg_hardstorage residency clear`](pg_hardstorage_residency_clear.md) | Remove residency constraint (allow any region) |
| [`pg_hardstorage residency list`](pg_hardstorage_residency_list.md) | Show every deployment's residency policy |
| [`pg_hardstorage residency set`](pg_hardstorage_residency_set.md) | Pin a deployment to one or more allowed regions |
| [`pg_hardstorage restore`](pg_hardstorage_restore.md) | Restore a backup to a target directory |
| [`pg_hardstorage rotate`](pg_hardstorage_rotate.md) | Apply retention policy to a deployment (or all) |
| [`pg_hardstorage runbook`](pg_hardstorage_runbook.md) | Generate a tailored runbook for the 3am operator |
| [`pg_hardstorage runbook generate`](pg_hardstorage_runbook_generate.md) | Render a runbook for <deployment> + scenario as Markdown |
| [`pg_hardstorage runbook list`](pg_hardstorage_runbook_list.md) | Enumerate available scenarios |
| [`pg_hardstorage schedule`](pg_hardstorage_schedule.md) | Set, show, or list deployment backup/rotate schedules |
| [`pg_hardstorage server`](pg_hardstorage_server.md) | Run the control plane |
| [`pg_hardstorage show`](pg_hardstorage_show.md) | Show details of a single backup |
| [`pg_hardstorage slo`](pg_hardstorage_slo.md) | RPO / RTO SLOs as code |
| [`pg_hardstorage slo clear`](pg_hardstorage_slo_clear.md) | Remove all SLO targets from a deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage slo report`](pg_hardstorage_slo_report.md) | Compare actual RPO against target; flag misses |
| [`pg_hardstorage slo set`](pg_hardstorage_slo_set.md) | Declare RPO / RTO targets for a deployment |
| [`pg_hardstorage slo show`](pg_hardstorage_slo_show.md) | Display configured SLO targets |
| [`pg_hardstorage standby`](pg_hardstorage_standby.md) | Hot-standby restore — read-only replica fed entirely from the backup pipeline |
| [`pg_hardstorage standby create`](pg_hardstorage_standby_create.md) | Provision a hot-standby data dir from a backup |
| [`pg_hardstorage standby destroy`](pg_hardstorage_standby_destroy.md) | Remove a standby from the state file (data dir kept by default) |
| [`pg_hardstorage standby list`](pg_hardstorage_standby_list.md) | List recorded standbys |
| [`pg_hardstorage status`](pg_hardstorage_status.md) | Show last-backup state for one or all deployments |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold`](pg_hardstorage_threshold.md) | k-of-n attestations: multi-party signing for highest-assurance manifests |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold attest`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_attest.md) | Sign / verify / show k-of-n attestations |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold attest show`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_attest_show.md) | Show one attestation: header + every signature with its validity |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold attest sign`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_attest_sign.md) | Add this operator's signature to an attestation (creates the header on first sign) |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold attest verify`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_attest_verify.md) | Verify the quorum on an attestation; exit 9 on quorum-not-met / tampering |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold roster`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_roster.md) | Create / list / show rosters |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold roster create`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_roster_create.md) | Admin-sign a new roster (refuses to overwrite an existing one) |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold roster list`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_roster_list.md) | List rosters in the repository |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold roster show`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_roster_show.md) | Show one roster's full body + members |
| [`pg_hardstorage threshold whoami`](pg_hardstorage_threshold_whoami.md) | Print this operator's roster-member identity (signer line for admins) |
| [`pg_hardstorage timetable`](pg_hardstorage_timetable.md) | Generate scheduling SQL for pg_timetable |
| [`pg_hardstorage timetable emit`](pg_hardstorage_timetable_emit.md) | Print (or directly install) pg_timetable SQL for the cron-friendly housekeeping commands |
| [`pg_hardstorage timetravel`](pg_hardstorage_timetravel.md) | Spin up an ephemeral read-only PG at a historical state |
| [`pg_hardstorage timetravel cleanup`](pg_hardstorage_timetravel_cleanup.md) | Reap expired timetravel sessions |
| [`pg_hardstorage timetravel create`](pg_hardstorage_timetravel_create.md) | Provision a timetravel session pinned at --at |
| [`pg_hardstorage timetravel destroy`](pg_hardstorage_timetravel_destroy.md) | Remove a session from the state file (data dir kept by default) |
| [`pg_hardstorage timetravel list`](pg_hardstorage_timetravel_list.md) | List timetravel sessions (active by default) |
| [`pg_hardstorage verify`](pg_hardstorage_verify.md) | Verify a backup's signature and chunks (no restore required) |
| [`pg_hardstorage version`](pg_hardstorage_version.md) | Print version information |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal`](pg_hardstorage_wal.md) | WAL transport: continuous streaming, push/fetch, list, repair |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal audit`](pg_hardstorage_wal_audit.md) | Detect WAL gaps and alarm on findings (cron-friendly) |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal fetch`](pg_hardstorage_wal_fetch.md) | Fetch one WAL segment from the repository (restore_command shim) |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal gap-purge`](pg_hardstorage_wal_gap-purge.md) | Reap orphan WAL-gap records (or every record for a wiped deployment) |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal gaps`](pg_hardstorage_wal_gaps.md) | List persisted WAL-gap records (Patroni-failover diagnostics) |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal list`](pg_hardstorage_wal_list.md) | List committed WAL segments in the repository |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal preflight`](pg_hardstorage_wal_preflight.md) | Validate PostgreSQL configuration for WAL streaming |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal prune`](pg_hardstorage_wal_prune.md) | Delete WAL segment manifests no kept backup can use for recovery |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal push`](pg_hardstorage_wal_push.md) | Archive one segment via archive_command (PG-invoked) |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal repair`](pg_hardstorage_wal_repair.md) | Recreate a missing replication slot; report any LSN gap |
| [`pg_hardstorage wal stream`](pg_hardstorage_wal_stream.md) | Continuously stream WAL into the repository |
